title: impatiens lemannii var. multiflora
intro: Impatiens lemannii var. multiflora, also known as the 'many-flowered jewelweed', is a plant species that belongs to the Balsam family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating skin irritations, wounds, and insect bites due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Impatiens lemannii var. multiflora include its ability to reduce swelling, promote healing, and provide relief from itching and pain. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
